sn3.4:1.2: As he was sitting to one side, King Pasenadi Kosala said to the Blessed One:
sn3.4:1.3: “Just now, lord, while I was alone in seclusion, this train of thought arose in my awareness:
sn3.4:1.4: ‘Who are dear to themselves, and who are not dear to themselves?’
sn3.4:1.6: ‘Those who engage in bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct
sn3.4:1.9: “We are dear to ourselves,” still they aren’t dear to themselves.
sn3.4:1.11: Of their own accord, they act toward themselves as an enemy would act toward an enemy;
sn3.4:1.12: thus they aren’t dear to themselves.
sn3.4:1.13: But those who engage in good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct
sn3.4:1.16: “We aren’t dear to ourselves,” still they are dear to themselves.
sn3.4:1.18: Of their own accord, they act toward themselves as a dear one would act toward a dear one;
sn3.4:1.19: thus they are dear to themselves.’”
sn3.4:2.1: “That’s the way it is, great king! That’s the way it is!
sn3.4:2.2: Those who engage in bodily misconduct, verbal misconduct, & mental misconduct
sn3.4:2.5: ‘We are dear to ourselves,’ still they aren’t dear to themselves.
sn3.4:2.7: Of their own accord, they act toward themselves as an enemy would act toward an enemy;
sn3.4:2.8: thus they aren’t dear to themselves.
sn3.4:2.9: But those who engage in good bodily conduct, good verbal conduct, & good mental conduct
sn3.4:2.13: still they are dear to themselves.
sn3.4:2.15: Of their own accord, they act toward themselves as a dear one would act toward a dear one;
sn3.4:2.16: thus they are dear to themselves.”
sn3.4:2.17: That is what the Blessed One said. Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn3.4:3.2: then don’t fetter yourself with evil,
sn3.4:3.4: by one who commits a wrong-doing.
sn3.4:4.2: as you abandon the human state,
sn3.4:4.4: What do you take along when you go?
sn3.4:4.6: like a shadow that never leaves?
sn3.4:5.2: that you as a mortal perform here:
sn3.4:5.4: what you take along when you go;
sn3.4:5.6: like a shadow that never leaves.
sn3.4:6.2: as an accumulation for the future life.
sn3.4:6.3: Deeds of merit are the support for beings
sn3.4:6.4: when they arise in the other world.”
